title: Rwanda Visa Application Form (Directorate General of Immigration and Emigration)
description: Rwanda's Directorate General of Immigration and Emigration (DGIE, an agency of the National Intelligence and Security Service, NISS)'s printed "Visa Application" form, used to apply for a Transit, Tourist, or Visitor's-Resident-Permanent visa at a diplomatic mission of Rwanda abroad. Opens Rwanda's Visa vertical (2 of 6); Rwanda's DMV vertical already exists in this registry at `rw/rra/vrf-e06-motor-vehicle-registration-form`. Most of Rwanda's visa issuance today routes through the IremboGov one-stop online portal (per DGIE's own "How to Apply for a Visa on IremboGov" guidance) or on-arrival at a border post, but this specimen — published by Rwanda's own diplomatic missions in the United Kingdom and the United States as the printed form applicants complete when applying in person or by mail at an embassy/consulate — remains a live, current issuance channel: migration.gov.rw's own "Visitors visa" page lists issuance "at the office of a diplomatic mission of Rwanda in the applicant's country of residence" alongside the border-post/online/immigration-office channels (see VERIFICATION.md for the exact quote and full currency check). The specimen is a flat, 2-page, non-fillable PDF (0 `/AcroForm` and 0 `/Widget` objects) — a print-and-hand-fill specimen, not a genuine AcroForm — so this is a structural reference derived from `pdfjs-dist` text/coordinate extraction, the same convention already used in this registry for `pe/sunat`, `uy/dgi`, and `ke/brs`. The form's 27 numbered items cover the applicant's identity, passport, family/parentage, marital status, and travel-history particulars; a printed "COLOURED PHOTO" box is modelled as a `documents[]` requirement rather than a field; a printed certification statement is modelled as a `documents[]` attestation. Item 27 ("Persons accompanying (children)") prints exactly 4 named child slots with no escape hatch to a companion form for a 5th+ child — this schema preserves that printed bound rather than inventing one. Item 19-22's spousal block (name/nationality/birth particulars/address of spouse) is gated via `requiredWhen` on marital status equalling "married". The top printed line ("Visa applied for: Transit / Tourist / Visitor's-Resident –Permanent" plus "Length of stay:") is modelled as an enum visa-type field plus a free-text length-of-stay field — see VERIFICATION.md for the disclosed judgment call on this line's punctuation and the absence of tick-box widgets on it (unlike the Gender and Marital Status questions, which do print an explicit "put a tick (√) in the relevant box" instruction and small checkbox rectangles). GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Rwanda or the Directorate General of Immigration and Emigration.

authority.name: Directorate General of Immigration and Emigration

source.documentRef: "Visa Application" form, Republic of Rwanda / National Intelligence and Security Service / Directorate General of Immigration and Emigration, 2-page flat (non-fillable) PDF

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle GOV-2544, a child of GOV-2542. Opens Rwanda's Visa vertical (2 of 6; DMV already exists at rw/rra/vrf-e06-motor-vehicle-registration-form). Fetched directly via curl from both cited mirrors: HTTP 200, application/pdf, 233,226 bytes each, sha256 01ffbc05db088ece045c25867f791892dc3b9eed808b1445867f3d29946378c0 — byte-identical across the rwandainuk.gov.rw and rwandainusa.gov.rw mirrors, independently re-derived this cycle rather than trusted. grep confirms 0 `/AcroForm` and 0 `/Widget` objects — a flat, print-and-hand-fill specimen. pdfjs-dist getAnnotations() independently confirms 0 annotations on both pages. Extraction was via pdfjs-dist getTextContent() (item text + x/y coordinates) plus getOperatorList() (rectangle/path drawing ops) to distinguish printed tick-box widgets from ordinary text-fill blank lines, for both pages. All 27 numbered items plus the two-part top line (visa type applied for / length of stay) were recovered and matched the pre-scouted summary exactly. Currency check: migration.gov.rw's own 'Visitors visa' page states visa issuance channels include 'at the office of a diplomatic mission of Rwanda in the applicant's country of residence,' alongside border-post/online/immigration-office channels — confirming this embassy-channel specimen remains current despite Rwanda's other verticals routing through IremboGov. Legal basis: Law N°57/2018 of 13/08/2018 on Immigration and Emigration in Rwanda, and Ministerial Order N°06/01 of 29/05/2019 relating to Immigration and Emigration, both published at migration.gov.rw/laws. 26 fields[] model items 1-27 (item 6 and item 16-18 each split into two fields per their two printed boxes); the spousal block (items 20-22) is requiredWhen-gated on marital status; item 27's 4 printed child slots are modelled as 4 bounded, independently-optional name/gender/date-of-birth triples with no 5th-child escape hatch. 2 documents[] entries capture the printed COLOURED PHOTO box (supporting-evidence) and the printed certification statement (attestation); the Signature line and 'Do not write below this line, for official use only' office footer are excluded from fields[]. See VERIFICATION.md for the full field inventory, every disclosed judgment call (including the top-line visa-type punctuation/tick-box-absence call and the contact-field requiredness call), and the conformance run write-up.

fields.name: visaTypeApplied
fields.label: Visa applied for
fields.description: The category of visa being applied for. Printed as a single slash-separated line with no tick-box widgets and no 'put a tick' instruction (unlike the Gender and Marital Status questions below, which explicitly print that instruction plus small checkbox rectangles) — modelled here as an enum of the form's own 3 printed options rather than free text, since the printed line unambiguously enumerates a closed set; see VERIFICATION.md for the disclosed judgment call on both this absence and the line's own odd 'Visitor's-Resident –Permanent' punctuation, preserved verbatim as this schema's third enum value.
fields.sourceRef: Page 1 top line: "Visa applied for: Transit / Tourist / Visitor's-Resident –Permanent"

fields.name: lengthOfStay
fields.label: Length of stay
fields.description: The requested duration of stay (e.g. "30 days"), printed as a short free-text blank beside the visa-type line. Not marked obligatory by the source.

fields.name: givenNames
fields.label: Given name(s)
fields.description: As shown in the applicant's passport.
fields.sourceRef: Item 1: "Given name(s)" (As shown in your passport)

fields.name: familyNames
fields.label: Family name(s)
fields.description: As shown in the applicant's passport.
fields.sourceRef: Item 2: "Family name(s)" (As shown in your passport)

fields.name: otherNames
fields.label: Other name(s)
fields.description: Any other names the applicant is or has been known by. Not marked obligatory by the source and inapplicable to an applicant with no other names.
fields.sourceRef: Item 3: "Other name(s) (including other names you are known by and/or other names that you have been known by)"

fields.name: gender
fields.label: Gender
fields.description: Applicant's gender, selected by ticking one of two printed boxes.
fields.sourceRef: Item 4: "Gender: put a tick (√) in the relevant box" — Male / Female

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of Birth
fields.description: Consolidated from the source's separate Day/Month/Year boxes into a single date field, per this registry's date-consolidation convention.

fields.name: placeOfBirthDistrict
fields.label: Place of Birth: District
fields.description: District of birth, printed as its own labelled box beside Country of birth.

fields.name: placeOfBirthCountry
fields.label: Place of Birth: Country
fields.description: Country of birth, printed as its own labelled box beside District of birth.

fields.name: emailContact
fields.label: E-mail contact
fields.description: Not marked obligatory by the source; the form gives no 'at least one of email/phone' instruction, unlike some other jurisdictions' forms in this registry, so both this field and localTelephoneContact are modelled as independently optional — a disclosed judgment call (see VERIFICATION.md).

fields.name: localTelephoneContact
fields.label: Local Telephone contact
fields.description: Not marked obligatory by the source. See emailContact for the at-least-one-of-two disclosure.

fields.name: passportIssueDate
fields.label: Date of Issue of passport
fields.description: Consolidated from the source's separate Day/Month/Year boxes into a single date field.

fields.name: passportExpiryDate
fields.label: Expiry date of passport
fields.description: Consolidated from the source's separate Day/Month/Year boxes into a single date field.

fields.name: employer
fields.label: Employer
fields.description: Not marked obligatory by the source; inapplicable to a self-employed, retired, or student applicant.

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital Status
fields.description: Selected by ticking one of four printed boxes.
fields.sourceRef: Item 19: "Marital Status: put a tick (√) in the relevant box" — Married / Widowed / Divorced / Never Married/single

fields.name: spouseName
fields.label: Name of spouse
fields.description: Only applicable, and modelled as required, when maritalStatus is "married" — a disclosed full-gating decision, not a partially-modelled pathway (see VERIFICATION.md).

fields.name: spouseBornDate
fields.label: Spouse's date of birth
fields.description: Consolidated from the source's separate Day/Month/Year boxes into a single date field.

fields.name: formerVisitsRwanda
fields.label: Former visits or stay in Rwanda
fields.description: Free-text description of any former visits or stay in Rwanda, and time of stay. Not marked obligatory by the source and inapplicable to a first-time visitor.
fields.sourceRef: Item 23: "Former visits or stay in Rwanda, and time of stay"

fields.name: formerStaysAfrica
fields.label: Former stays in Africa
fields.description: Free-text description of former stays in Africa, places, and dates. Not marked obligatory by the source and inapplicable to an applicant with no such history.
fields.sourceRef: Item 24: "Former stays in Africa, places, and dates"

fields.name: proposedPersonsToVisit
fields.label: Proposed persons to be visited and their address
fields.sourceRef: Item 26: "Proposed persons to be visited and their address"

fields.name: child1Name
fields.label: Persons accompanying (child 1): Name
fields.description: The form prints exactly 4 named child slots with no escape hatch to a companion form for a 5th+ accompanying child; this schema preserves that printed bound rather than inventing one. All 3 fields of each child slot are modelled as independently optional, rather than requiredWhen-gating gender/date-of-birth on the name being present, to avoid this registry's documented notEquals-empty-string/absent-optional-field anti-pattern — a disclosed simplification.

fields.name: child1Gender
fields.label: Persons accompanying (child 1): Gender
fields.sourceRef: Item 27: "Persons accompanying (children): put a tick (√) in the relevant box" — child slot 1, "Gender:" Male / Female

documents.label: Coloured photo (passport-style photo, printed as a labelled box on Page 1 beside the applicant's identity questions)
Certification of accuracy
documents.statement: I hereby certify that all information is complete and correct
documents.sourceRef: Page 2: certification statement above the Signature line
